title: Metal Magic
description: Glam metal debut released on the band's own Metal Magic Records label. Produced by Jerry Abbott at Pantego Studios.

title: Projects in the Jungle
description: Second album showing heavier Judas Priest influences while remaining rooted in glam metal.

title: I Am the Night
description: Third album with improved production and a heavier sound. Sold around 25,000 copies.

title: Power Metal
description: First album with Phil Anselmo. A transitional record blending glam, traditional metal, and thrash. The band ditches spandex for a more authentic image.

title: Cowboys from Hell
description: Major-label debut on Atco Records and the birth of groove metal. The band's self-declared 'official' debut. Certified 2x Platinum. Features 'Cemetery Gates', hailed as one of metal's greatest ballads.

title: Vulgar Display of Power
description: The album that cemented Pantera's status as groove metal pioneers. 'Walk' became one of metal's most recognizable anthems. Certified 2x Platinum.

title: Far Beyond Driven
description: Debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 -- a rare feat for an extreme metal album. Features 'I'm Broken' and a Black Sabbath cover of 'Planet Caravan'. Certified Platinum.

title: The Great Southern Trendkill
description: Recorded with the band members in separate studios due to internal tensions. Features the acclaimed guitar solo on 'Floods'. Certified Platinum.

title: Reinventing the Steel
description: Pantera's final studio album. A back-to-basics approach that peaked at #4 on the Billboard 200. Certified Gold.
